title: Qualification Details
text: following information collected display PDF report IQ process: R installation location R welcome banner System user information Platform specific variables R software version information Numerical characteristics machine Session information Package library location Pandoc version information version installation path TinyTex

title: Qualification Details
text: OQ, functions testInstalledBasic testInstalledPackages base tools package used check functionality base recommended packages. following 7 checks performed: System test execution via testInstalledBasic("") Base package examples execution via testInstalledPackages(scope="base", types="examples") Base package vignettes execution via testInstalledPackages(scope="base", types="vignettes") Recommended package examples execution via testInstalledPackages(scope="recommended", types="examples") Recommended package vignettes execution via testInstalledPackages(scope="recommended", types="vignettes") Base package tests execution via testInstalledPackages(scope="base", types="tests") Recommended package tests execution via testInstalledPackages(scope="recommended", types="tests") check result package function displayed section PDF report, along error messages encountered execution. case IQ OQ, error captured displayed PDF report, along message indicating check failed.

title: Qualification Details
text: following example PDF report generated rqualify() function, includes results IQ OQ checks, well summary table checks performed outcomes.

title: Introduction to the rqualify Package
text: vignette overview R-validation.Rmd file, showing code executed file rendered. overview informational purposes documents internal structure R-validation.Rmd file. code blocks updated, considered breaking change document still render properly. Please see https://medtronic-biostatistics.github.io/rqualify/index.html full documentation. minimal usage example:
code: library(rqualify)  # Execute qualification using default arguments rqualify(path_save = tempdir())

title: Introduction to the rqualify Package
text: includes example output installation facts, installation qualification, operational qualification. installation facts qualification results generated time package build site build, operational qualification results generated Windows machine R version 4.5.1.

title: Introduction to the rqualify Package
text: First, R installation facts gathered stored variables. variables used populate title page report details R installation qualified.
code: # Get R installation facts RVersionInfo <- R.Version() Version      <- RVersionInfo$version.string Arch         <- gsub("_", " ", RVersionInfo$arch) Platform     <- gsub("_", " ", RVersionInfo$platform) R Version: R version 4.5.3 (2026-03-11)  Architecture: x86 64  Platform: x86 64-pc-linux-gnu

title: Introduction to the rqualify Package
text: following output R.home(), showing R installed computer: following output system("R -e 'q()'"), presenting R welcome banner displayed default R console (terminal) show R console correctly running exiting: following output Sys.info(), defining details current system upon R running user information: following output .Platform, defining details platform upon R built (compiled): following output R.version, defining detailed information currently running version R: following output .Machine, defining numerical characteristics computer upon R running: following output sessionInfo(), defining current R version, locale information attached packages: following output .libPaths(), current package library location; may one folder: following output rmarkdown::pandoc_version() listing version Pandoc used render report: following output tinytex::tlmgr_version() listing version installation path TinyTex used render report pdf:
code: r_home <- paste0(R.home(), sep="\n") /opt/R/4.5.3/lib/R # Output the R startup banner results0 <- try(system(paste(shQuote(file.path(R.home("bin"), "R")), "-e", shQuote("q()")), intern = TRUE))  if (class(results0) != "try-error"){   results0 <- paste(results0, collapse = "\n")   results0 <- gsub("> q\\(\\)", "", results0) } else{   results0 <- "Unable to execute R at the command line" } R version 4.5.3 (2026-03-11) -- "Reassured Reassurer" Copyright (C) 2026 The R Foundation for Statistical Computing Platform: x86_64-pc-linux-gnu  R is free software and comes with ABSOLUTELY NO WARRANTY. You are welcome to redistribute it under certain conditions. Type 'license()' or 'licence()' for distribution details.  R is a collaborative project with many contributors. Type 'demo()' for some demos, 'help()' for on-line help, or 'help.start()' for an HTML browser interface to help. Type 'q()' to quit R. # Run Sys.info() at the command line results_sysinfo <- code_exec(code_block    = "Sys.info()",                              file_prefix   = "sysinfo",                              folder_output = dir_temp)  # Clean-up the results (remove the unnecessary preamble and arrow) results_sysinfo_clean <- results_sysinfo[-(1:(which(results_sysinfo == "> Sys.info()")))]  if(any(results_sysinfo_clean == "> ")){   results_sysinfo_clean <- results_sysinfo_clean[-which(results_sysinfo_clean == "> ")] } sysname                                               "Linux"                                               release                                   "6.17.0-1008-azure"                                               version  "#8~24.04.1-Ubuntu SMP Mon Jan 26 18:35:40 UTC 2026"                                              nodename                                       "runnervmrg6be"                                               machine                                              "x86_64"                                                 login                                             "unknown"                                                  user                                              "runner"                                        effective_user                                              "runner" # Run .Platform at the command line results_platform <- code_exec(code_block    = ".Platform",                               file_prefix   = "platform",                               folder_output = dir_temp)  # Clean-up the results (remove the unnecessary preamble) results_platform_clean <- results_platform[-(1:(which(results_platform == "> .Platform")))]  if(any(results_platform_clean == "> ")){   results_platform_clean <- results_platform_clean[-which(results_platform_clean == "> ")] } $OS.type [1] "unix"  $file.sep [1] "/"  $dynlib.ext [1] ".so"  $GUI [1] "X11"  $endian [1] "little"  $pkgType [1] "source"  $path.sep [1] ":"  $r_arch [1] "" # Run R.version at the command line results_rversion <- code_exec(code_block    = "R.version",                               file_prefix   = "rversion",                               folder_output = dir_temp)  # Clean-up the results (remove the unnecessary preamble) results_rversion_clean <- results_rversion[-(1:(which(results_rversion == "> R.version")))]  if(any(results_rversion_clean == "> ")){   results_rversion_clean <- results_rversion_clean[-which(results_rversion_clean == "> ")] } _                            platform       x86_64-pc-linux-gnu          arch           x86_64                       os             linux-gnu                    system         x86_64, linux-gnu            status                                      major          4                            minor          5.3                          year           2026                         month          03                           day            11                           svn rev        89597                        language       R                            version.string R version 4.5.3 (2026-03-11) nickname       Reassured Reassurer # Run .Machine at the command line results_machine <- code_exec(code_block    = ".Machine",                              file_prefix   = "machine",                              folder_output = dir_temp)  # Clean-up the results (remove the unnecessary preamble) results_machine_clean <- results_machine[-(1:(which(results_machine == "> .Machine")))]  if(any(results_machine_clean == "> ")){   results_machine_clean <- results_machine_clean[-which(results_machine_clean == "> ")] } $double.eps [1] 2.220446e-16  $double.neg.eps [1] 1.110223e-16  $double.xmin [1] 2.225074e-308  $double.xmax [1] 1.797693e+308  $double.base [1] 2  $double.digits [1] 53  $double.rounding [1] 5  $double.guard [1] 0  $double.ulp.digits [1] -52  $double.neg.ulp.digits [1] -53  $double.exponent [1] 11  $double.min.exp [1] -1022  $double.max.exp [1] 1024  $integer.max [1] 2147483647  $sizeof.long [1] 8  $sizeof.longlong [1] 8  $sizeof.longdouble [1] 16  $sizeof.pointer [1] 8  $sizeof.time_t [1] 8  $longdouble.eps [1] 1.084202e-19  $longdouble.neg.eps [1] 5.421011e-20  $longdouble.digits [1] 64  $longdouble.rounding [1] 5  $longdouble.guard [1] 0  $longdouble.ulp.digits [1] -63  $longdouble.neg.ulp.digits [1] -64  $longdouble.exponent [1] 15  $longdouble.min.exp [1] -16382  $longdouble.max.exp [1] 16384 # Clean-up the results (remove the unnecessary preamble) results_sessioninfo_clean <- results_sessioninfo[-(1:(which(results_sessioninfo == "> sessionInfo()")))]  if(any(results_sessioninfo_clean == "> ")){   results_sessioninfo_clean <- results_sessioninfo_clean[-which(results_sessioninfo_clean == "> ")] } R version 4.5.3 (2026-03-11) Platform: x86_64-pc-linux-gnu Running under: Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS  Matrix products: default BLAS:   /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/openblas-pthread/libblas.so.3  LAPACK: /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/openblas-pthread/libopenblasp-r0.3.26.so;  LAPACK version 3.12.0  locale:  [1] LC_CTYPE=C.UTF-8       LC_NUMERIC=C           LC_TIME=C.UTF-8         [4] LC_COLLATE=C.UTF-8     LC_MONETARY=C.UTF-8    LC_MESSAGES=C.UTF-8     [7] LC_PAPER=C.UTF-8       LC_NAME=C              LC_ADDRESS=C           [10] LC_TELEPHONE=C         LC_MEASUREMENT=C.UTF-8 LC_IDENTIFICATION=C     time zone: UTC tzcode source: system (glibc)  attached base packages: [1] stats     graphics  grDevices utils     datasets  methods   base       loaded via a namespace (and not attached): [1] compiler_4.5.3 results_libpath <- code_exec(code_block   = ".libPaths()",                              file_prefix  = "libpaths",                              folder_output = dir_temp)  # what: R Core Operational Qualification - System Tests (OQ)
title: Introduction to the rqualify Package
text: following output testInstalledBasic(""), runs series core system-wide operational tests R installation, including various regression tests: final line output displays status running tests. PASS indicates successful running tests, FAIL indicate error detected running tests. may tests result performing diff two files compared demonstrate content difference may may relevant may dependent upon locale settings. differences displayed output reviewed detail determine relevance Operational Qualification R installation.
code:
# Copy system tests to IQ-OQ-TestOutput/tests r_test_path <- file.path(R.home(), "tests") fc          <- file.copy(r_test_path, "IQ-OQ-TestOutput", recursive=TRUE)  # Set absolute test path path_system_tests <- normalizePath("IQ-OQ-TestOutput/tests", winslash="/")  code_check1 <- sprintf(' options(echo = FALSE) options(useFancyQuotes = FALSE)  Failure <- tryCatch(tools:::testInstalledBasic(                     scope      = "basic",                     outDir     = "%s",                     testSrcdir = "%s"                     ),                     error=function(e) TRUE)  if (Failure){   cat("

Test suite result: FAIL

")   fc <- file.create("IQ-OQ-TestOutput/CMDFile1Fail", showWarnings = FALSE) } else {   cat("

Test suite result: PASS

") } q(status = Failure) ',path_system_tests,path_system_tests)  results1 <- code_exec(code_block    = code_check1,                       file_prefix   = "CMDFile1",                       folder_output = "IQ-OQ-TestOutput") R version 4.5.1 (2025-06-13 ucrt) -- "Great Square Root" Copyright (C) 2025 The R Foundation for Statistical Computing Platform: x86_64-w64-mingw32/x64  R is free software and comes with ABSOLUTELY NO WARRANTY. title: Introduction to rqualify
text: rqualify function performs following steps: Creates folder tree R-validation/IQ-OQ-TestOutput specified path_save. Installs TinyTeX necessary LaTeX packages (setup_tinytex=TRUE). Installs Pandoc (setup_pandoc=TRUE). Copies R-validation.Rmd R-validation folder. Executes IQ-OQ rendering R-validation.Rmd R-validation.tex. Compiles R-validation.tex R-validation.pdf. case developing process user-level qualification, file R-validation.Rmd located root directory package qualify_r/R-validation.Rmd can used template validation document. Note, viewing Github, R-validation.Rmd file located inst/qualify_r/R-validation.Rmd.

title: Run IQ-OQ on an installation of R software — rqualify
text: path_save Character. Path save R-validation folder. Ensure folder named `R-validation` already exist location. setup_tinytex Logical. TRUE, sets TinyTeX LaTeX document generation. Note, install tinytex R package, TinyTeX LaTeX bundle. convenient wrapper installing TinyTeX using `tinytex::install_tinytex()`, adding TinyTeX location environment. function installs "TinyTeX" bundle additional package `grfext`, sets TinyTeX installation path system PATH. setup_pandoc Logical. TRUE, sets pandoc document conversion. Note, install pandoc R package, Pandoc software. convenient wrapper around `pandoc::pandoc_install()` `pandoc::pandoc_activate()`, called internally. render_latex Logical. TRUE, renders generated LaTeX file PDF using `tinytex::pdflatex()`. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress messages console.

title: Run IQ-OQ on an installation of R software — rqualify
code: if (FALSE) { # tinytex::is_tinytex() && pandoc::pandoc_available() # \donttest{ # Render the R-validation report, must have TinyTeX and Pandoc installed for  # this example, otherwise set setup_tinytex and setup_pandoc to TRUE. rqualify(path_save     = tempdir(),          setup_tinytex = FALSE,          setup_pandoc  = FALSE) # } DONTSHOW({ unlink(file.path(tempdir(), "R-validation"), recursive=TRUE) })    }
